children.text: grants for energy audits and renewable energy development assistance; and
financial assistance for energy efficiency improvements and renewable energy systems.

children.heading: Energy audits and renewable energy development assistance
children.label: b Energy audits and renewable energy development assistance
A
children.text: to become more energy efficient; and

children.label: B
children.text: to use renewable energy technologies and resources.

children.label: A
children.text: a unit of State, tribal, or local government;

children.label: B
children.text: a land-grant college or university or other institution of higher education;

children.label: C
children.text: a rural electric cooperative or public power entity;

children.label: D
children.text: a council (as defined in ); andsection 3451 of title 16

children.label: E
children.text: any other similar entity, as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: the ability and expertise of the eligible entity in providing professional energy audits and renewable energy assessments;

children.label: B
children.text: the geographic scope of the program proposed by the eligible entity in relation to the identified need;

children.label: C
children.text: the number of agricultural producers and rural small businesses to be assisted by the program;

children.label: D
children.text: the potential of the proposed program to produce energy savings and environmental benefits;

children.label: E
children.text: the plan of the eligible entity for performing outreach and providing information and assistance to agricultural producers and rural small businesses on the benefits of energy efficiency and renewable energy development; and

children.label: F
children.text: the ability of the eligible entity to leverage other sources of funding.

children.label: A
children.text: conducting and promoting energy audits; and

children.label: i
children.text: to improve the energy efficiency of the operations of the agricultural producers and rural small businesses; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to use renewable energy technologies and resources in the operations.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 5 Limitation
children.text: Grant recipients may not use more than 5 percent of a grant for administrative expenses.

children.heading: Cost sharing
children.label: 6 Cost sharing
children.text: A recipient of a grant under paragraph (1) that conducts an energy audit for an agricultural producer or rural small business under paragraph (4) shall require that, as a condition of the energy audit, the agricultural producer or rural small business pay at least 25 percent of the cost of the energy audit, which shall be retained by the eligible entity for the cost of the energy audit.

children.heading: Financial assistance for energy efficiency improvements and renewable energy systems
children.label: c Financial assistance for energy efficiency improvements and renewable energy systems
I
children.text: to purchase renewable energy systems, including systems that may be used to produce and sell electricity; and

children.label: II
children.text: to make energy efficiency improvements; and

children.label: I
children.text: energy efficiency building codes, if applicable;

children.label: II
children.text: Federal or State energy efficiency standards, if applicable; and

children.label: III
children.text: other energy efficiency standards determined appropriate by the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: if no codes or standards described in such subparagraph apply to the energy efficient equipment or system to be purchased or installed pursuant to such subparagraph, the Secretary shall require, to the maximum extent practicable, such equipment or system to meet the same efficiency measurements as the most efficient available equipment or system in the market; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the Secretary shall not provide such a loan guarantee for the purchase or installation of any energy efficient equipment or system unless more than one type of such equipment or system is available in the market.

children.label: A
children.text: the type of renewable energy system to be purchased;

children.label: B
children.text: the estimated quantity of energy to be generated by the renewable energy system;

children.label: C
children.text: the expected environmental benefits of the renewable energy system;

children.label: D
children.text: the quantity of energy savings expected to be derived from the activity, as demonstrated by an energy audit;

children.label: E
children.text: the estimated period of time for the energy savings generated by the activity to equal the cost of the activity;

children.label: F
children.text: the expected energy efficiency of the renewable energy system; and

children.label: G
children.text: other appropriate factors.

children.heading: Grants
children.label: A Grants
children.text: The amount of a grant under this subsection shall not exceed 25 percent of the cost of the activity carried out using funds from the grant.

children.heading: Maximum amount of loan guarantees
children.label: B Maximum amount of loan guarantees
children.text: The amount of a loan guaranteed under this subsection shall not exceed $25,000,000.

children.heading: Maximum amount of combined grant and loan guarantee
children.label: C Maximum amount of combined grant and loan guarantee
children.text: The combined amount of a grant and loan guaranteed under this subsection shall not exceed 75 percent of the cost of the activity funded under this subsection.

children.heading: Loan guarantees for energy efficient equipment to agricultural producers
children.label: D Loan guarantees for energy efficient equipment to agricultural producers
children.text: Using funds made available under paragraphs (1) and (3) of subsection (f), in each fiscal year the Secretary may use for loan guarantees under paragraph (1)(A)(ii) an amount that does not exceed 15 percent of such funds.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: In providing loan guarantees and grants under this subsection, the Secretary shall use a 3-tiered application process that reflects the size of proposed projects in accordance with this paragraph.

children.heading: Tier 1
children.label: B Tier 1
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a separate application process for projects for which the cost of the activity funded under this subsection is not more than $80,000.

children.heading: Tier 2
children.label: C Tier 2
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a separate application process for projects for which the cost of the activity funded under this subsection is greater than $80,000 but less than $200,000.

children.heading: Tier 3
children.label: D Tier 3
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a separate application process for projects for which the cost of the activity funded under this subsection is equal to or greater than $200,000.

children.heading: Application process
children.label: E Application process
children.text: The Secretary shall establish an application, evaluation, and oversight process that is the most simplified for tier I projects and more comprehensive for each subsequent tier.

children.heading: Outreach
children.label: d Outreach
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure, to the maximum extent practicable, that adequate outreach relating to this section is being conducted at the State and local levels.

children.heading: Limitation on use of funds
children.label: 1 Limitation on use of funds
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), the Secretary shall use not less than 20 percent of the funds made available under subsection (f) to provide grants of $20,000 or less.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 2 Exception
children.text: Effective beginning on June 30 of each fiscal year, paragraph (1) shall not apply to funds made available under subsection (f) for the fiscal year.

children.label: A
children.text: $55,000,000 for fiscal year 2009;

children.label: B
children.text: $60,000,000 for fiscal year 2010;

children.label: C
children.text: $70,000,000 for fiscal year 2011;

children.label: D
children.text: $70,000,000 for fiscal year 2012; and

children.label: E
children.text: $50,000,000 for fiscal year 2014 and each fiscal year thereafter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), of the funds made available for each fiscal year under paragraph (1), 4 percent shall be available to carry out subsection (b).

children.heading: Other use
children.label: B Other use
children.text: Funds not obligated under subparagraph (A) by April 1 of each fiscal year to carry out subsection (b) shall become available to carry out subsection (c).

children.heading: Discretionary funding
children.label: 3 Discretionary funding
children.text: In addition to any other funds made available to carry out this section, there is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $20,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2019 through 2023.
